results.title: Senior Analyst, Onboard Revenue | Remotepulse
highlights:
# Senior Analyst, Onboard Revenue
...
This position is classified as “in-office.” As an in-office role, it requires employees to work from a designated Carnival office in South Florida Tuesday through Thursday each week. Employees may work from their homes on Mondays and Fridays. Candidates must be located in (or willing to relocate to) the Miami/Ft. Lauderdale area. The Onboard Revenue Senior Analyst is responsible for leveraging data analytics and advanced modeling techniques to drive revenue optimization and enhance the guest experience across onboard operations. It involves analyzing complex datasets, developing predictive models, and maintaining robust data infrastructure to support strategic decision-making. The Onboard Revenue Data Scientist will work closely with revenue management, onboard operations, marketing, finance, and IT teams to uncover insights, streamline processes, and implement data-driven solutions that align with business goals. The ideal candidate will have strong analytical skills, experience with machine learning and data visualization, and a collaborative mindset to thrive in a cross-functional environment.
...
- Researching and prototyping machine learning models aimed at improving demand forecasting, dynamic pricing, and guest behavior prediction.
...
- Staying informed on emerging AI technologies and evaluating their potential applications within cruise revenue operations is a continuous responsibility.
...
- Bachelor’s degree in data science, Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Economics, Engineering or related quantitative field
...
+ years of experience
...
- Familiarity with data visualization tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or similar platforms
...
- Basic knowledge of machine learning algorithms and their applications
...
- Experience creating compelling data visualizations and presentations
...
- Ability to create compelling data visualizations and presentations

highlights:
Senior Analyst, Revenue Management Strategy job at Carnival Cruise Lines in

# Senior Analyst, Revenue Management Strategy job at Carnival Cruise Lines in Miami, FL
...
Princess Cruises operates an advanced
...
020. This system
...
recommendations which assist

brands within Carnival Corporation.
...
YODA Project Management: Supporting the Revenue Management Strategy Senior Director on the implementation of YODA for all the participating brands. This includes developing hypotheses, identifying potential opportunities to leverage new enabling capabilities (e.g., generative AI), and contributing to project plans. Once a project is formulated, you must also contribute to the creation of RFPs, support the tracking of available resources and monitor how project objectives are met through project deliverables. During a project, track the progress of developments and ensure it is aligned to agreed prioritization. Feedback updates on project status to Revenue Management Strategy Senior Director, highlighting areas of concern. Understand the processes by which issues are raised and triaged, supporting appropriate delegation of issues. In order to contribute effectively to this project, ad hoc travel to all of our brand office locations will be required. Additionally, work hours will need to be adjusted to provide overlap with the brand locations.
...
role involves assisting with pricing strategies, inventory analysis, and demand forecasting to help maximize profitability across several brands within Carnival
...
team leads and managers in revenue management and may
...
to presentations for
...
overall business objectives
...
ideal candidate will
...
experience in revenue
...
Problem-solving: The Senior Analyst, Revenue Management Strategy is expected to support the resolution of revenue optimization challenges across multiple cruise line brands. This role requires the ability to work with large datasets, identify key patterns, and assist in the development of initiatives to increase revenue. The individual will help monitor market trends, competitor activity, and customer preferences to contribute to effective pricing and inventory management recommendations. Additionally, the Senior Business Analyst will collaborate with various departments to help implement solutions that support overall business objectives and profitability.
...
Impact: The Senior Analyst, Revenue Management Strategy plays a supporting role in driving the financial performance of the company by contributing to revenue management strategies. Working as part of the team responsible for delivering at least 1% yield impact per year, this position helps ensure the achievement of these goals. The Senior Business Analyst impacts the business by supporting the optimization of pricing, inventory, and promotional strategies to maximize revenue and profitability. The role also involves working with other departments to support alignment with company objectives and using data analytics to inform recommendations and decision-making processes
...
Leadership: As a Senior Analyst within the Revenue Management Strategy team, you will support the strategic direction set by senior leadership and contribute to the success of the global YODA teams. Your responsibilities include collaborating with colleagues to foster a results-driven and cooperative environment and helping to ensure that team efforts are aligned with the company's revenue objectives. You will play a role in managing resources and budgets under guidance, utilizing data to inform your work, and supporting initiatives that enhance business performance. This position involves partnering primrily with business teams but also with data science teams to support cross-functional projects and help drive optimal revenue outcomes

results.title: Carnival AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Carnival Corporation, the world's largest cruise company, has established itself as a measured innovator in AI adoption, balancing technological advancement with operational discipline. The company has piloted over 100 generative AI projects but has only deployed six into full production, demonstrating a cautious approach focused on practical business impact [1][2]. Carnival's AI initiatives span multiple areas including enhanced guest experience through digital transformation, operational efficiency through AI-assisted fuel consumption management, and smart ship infrastructure that enables real-time personalization and better fleet coordination [3]. CEO Josh Weinstein positioned 2026 as 'the year where tinkering becomes transformation,' signaling a shift from experimental AI to practical business implementation [3]. The company has also formed strategic partnerships, including with DXC Technology for comprehensive IT infrastructure management and with AI provider Quvia (formerly Neuron) to enhance onboard connectivity using AI-powered platforms [4][5].
...

## Frequently Asked Questions
...
How many AI projects has Carnival tested?
...
Carnival has piloted over 100 generative AI projects, though only 6 are currently in full production as of September 2025.
...
What is Carnival's approach to AI implementation?
...
Carnival takes a measured approach with an AI governing body that meets monthly to evaluate projects, focusing on practical business impact rather than having 'science experiments everywhere.'
...
What AI technologies is Carnival using for operations?
...
Carnival uses AI for fuel consumption optimization, kitchen intelligence, connectivity management, and operational efficiency across its fleet of 29 ships.
...
Who leads Carnival's AI initiatives?
...
Sean Kenny, who has served as CIO since 2017, leads Carnival's AI initiatives with support from an established AI governing body and partnerships with major tech companies.

Data scientist with over 7 years' experience in using advanced analytics and machine learning techniques to solve business problems and generate actionable insights that drive commercial value. Experience developing and implementing a range of models including supervised machine learning (classification and regression), clustering, NLP, recommendation engines and customer segmentation. highlights:
Data Specialist at [Carnival Cruise Line](https://www.linkedin.com/company/carnival-cruise-lines)
...
Data Specialist with a proven track record in data science, analytics, and machine learning, offering over 4 years of expertise in transforming complex datasets into actionable insights. I bring a robust background in SQL, Python, and cloud-based data solutions, combined with hands-on experience in ETL/ELT process design and data pipeline optimization. My recent role at Florida International University (FIU) honed my skills in developing machine learning models, constructing data processes, and driving decision-making through custom Power BI dashboards, significantly improving data quality and reporting efficiency. Previously, as a Data Scientist at Wipro Technologies, I optimized data models, developed scalable data solutions with PySpark and Azure Data Factory, and increased stakeholder engagement by 40% through interactive dashboards. Skilled in Agile and Scrum methodologies, I effectively accelerated project delivery while consistently exceeding client expectations with 95% satisfaction ratings. Passionate about leveraging data for innovation, I have led predictive modeling projects in healthcare, including breast cancer diagnostics and stroke risk assessment, integrating clinical insights with cutting-edge AI to improve patient outcomes. My work in app trend analysis on the Google Playstore demonstrates my ability to provide actionable market insights. I am continually advancing my technical toolkit, holding certifications in Microsoft Azure and Google Cloud, with expertise in Alteryx and Power BI Data Flows. results.title: Roger Rodrigues
highlights:
Data & Analytics Engineer at [Carnival Maritime GmbH](https://www.linkedin.com/company/carnival-maritime-gmbh)
...
➔ Certified Data Scientist with +3 years of experience in the development and deployment of Machine Learning models for problem solving in the industrial environment ➔ Solid background in idealizing and developing technical solutions combining industrial engineering and data science technical knowledge ➔ Coordinated initiatives and studies to define and prioritize Data Science projects and solutions inspired by business problems and needs ➔ Experienced in Agile and Hybrid methodologies as a team member ➔ Fluent in Portuguese and English; good knowledge of German Hi! My name is Roger, and this is my professional background in a nutshell. I'm a MSc in Mechanical Engineering. The major subjects of my academic background were related to Vibration Analysis and Signal Processing. At the end of my academic journey, I decided to follow the career of a Data Scientist. I know that it may sound like a turbulent change of career, but it was more natural than seems. Did I have to learn many things? Yes, indeed a lot! However, I was highly skilled in programming and I had extensive theoretical knowledge, gathered during my Bachelor's and Master's degrees. Those factors allowed me to become an interesting profile for Data Scientist in an Industrial company. So, aside from knowing how machines and sensors work, I needed to learn the principles and tools that would allow the use of Data Science in the company. And the last two years of my professional experience was a fast pace learning on Digital Transformation and Applied Data Science. I was able to learn and use the languages Python and SQL; the well-known packages for data processing and model development: Scikit-Learn, Pandas, and Numpy; for data visualization and dashboards development: Matplotlib, Seaborn, and the great Dash Plotly; for model deployment: Flask, FastAPI, Windows Server and IIS; for version control: Git and Azure DevOps; for ETL and BI: Microsoft SQL Integrated Services and Power BI; and for Management Project: Agile Framework. The tools mentioned were all used in real projects, which details you may see in the role description available in my profile. In addition to the packages referred above, I also had the opportunity to learn Docker, for containerization of our projects; Kedro as a framework for standardizing; unit testing for code proofing. The idea was to use those tools in the next step, to make our projects achieve a higher level of availability and scalability, mainly the projects used at the OT level. And that is the short version of the practical experience I have.
...

# Associate CASS Data Scientist
...
As an Associate CASS Data Scientist, you’ll help drive the analytical backbone of Southwest’s Continuing Analysis and Surveillance System (CASS), turning complex operational data into insights that strengthen safety, compliance, and reliability. You’ll develop automated workflows, build models, and apply advanced analytics—including machine learning and AI—to detect trends, assess risk, and optimize the health of our Continuous Airworthiness Maintenance Program. Partnering closely with Tech Ops, CASS Leadership, and cross‑functional stakeholders, you’ll create dashboards and predictive tools that inform critical decisions and support the ongoing improvement of our maintenance programs. By blending strong analytical methods with real‑world operational understanding, you’ll play a key role in ensuring our fleet stays safe, efficient, and ready to serve our Customers.

results.title: Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
highlights:
Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization | CareerSync
...
# Principal Machine Learning Engineer- LLM Fine-tuning and Optimization
...
Machine Learning and Artificial Intelligence are at the heart of the Airbnb product. From Trust to Payments, and from Customer Service to Marketing we rely on ML to ensure that guests and hosts have the best possible experience with Airbnb.
...
The CS AI product team is responsible for driving CSxAI (Customer Support x Artificial Intelligence) initiatives by adopting the Generative AI technologies to enable an intelligent, scalable and exceptional service experience. The team develops and enhances various AI models, ML services and tools including LLM fine-tuning, alignment and optimization, RAG/Search, LLM evaluation and testing automation, feedback-based learning and guardrail for a wide range of applications in Airbnb.
...
As a principal machine learning engineer, you will be responsible for fine-tuning state-of-the-art LLMs for diverse use cases while optimizing models for high-performance deployment on Airbnb’s ML Infrastructure. You will partner with product managers, software engineers, data scientists and operation teams to brainstorm, design and develop AI products such as AI Assistant, Autonomous agent, recommendation, travel planning, and many more products that make meaningful impacts in the world of travel.
...
- Work with large scale structured and unstructured data; explore, experiment, build and continuously improve foundation models for Airbnb product, business and operational use cases.
...
- Create a multi-year tech roadmap that enables our team to stay on the leading edge of the rapidly evolving AI landscape and leverage the best in class technologies to deliver customer benefits.
...
- Continuously evaluate recent and upcoming large foundational models, ensuring the selection and refinement of the highest quality models for enhanced performance and efficiency.
...
- Hands-on prototype, develop and productionize LLM models and pipelines at scale, including both batch and real-time use cases.
...
- Drive key AI architectural decisions for products, and contribute to Airbnb’s ML platform architecture and strategy.
...
- Minimum Qualifications:
...
- PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics, Statistics, or related technical field.
- 10+ years of experience with developing machine learning models and products at scale from inception to business impact.
...
- Programming experience in Python and hands-on experience with frameworks such as PyTorch.
...
- Proven record of training, fine tuning, optimizing models and inference run-time
...
- Post-training experience in areas like data processing for fine-tuning; responsible LLMs; LLM alignment; reinforcement learning; efficient training and inference; language model evaluation; and/or multilingual and multimodal modeling.
...
- Or specialized experience in runtime optimizations, model quantization, compression, on-device inference, GPU inference, pytorch, kernel development
...
- Preferred Qualifications:
...
- PhD in AI, machine learning, data science, or related technical fields.
...
- Publications at peer-reviewed AI conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, CVPR, ICML, ICLR, ICCV, and ACL).
...
- Customer Support Systems: Experience with AI technologies in customer support applications.
...
- Agile Practice for AI production: Experience with the entire AI product development lifecycle from incubation to production at scale, following agile practices in the Applied AI/ML domain.
...
- Infrastructure Acumen: Experience deploying and scaling business-critical AI services and driving architectural requirements on ML infrastructures

results.title: Senior Data Scientist, Platform | Wfhforgeon
highlights:
Senior Data Scientist, Platform | Wfhforgeon
...
# Senior Data Scientist, Platform
...
Trust is the foundation of a vibrant Airbnb community. The Content Integrity Data Science team helps build and protect that trust by ensuring that the content on Airbnb, including listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated experiences, is accurate, authentic and aligned with our policies and community standards.
...
We partner closely with product, engineering, policy and operations teams to develop advanced AI-driven content understanding systems, scale effective human-in-the-loop workflows and proactively defend against emerging content risks, so guests and hosts can interact with confidence.
...
This role is unique in that it directly improves the safety, trust, and quality of real-world user experiences by advancing Airbnb’s ability to understand, interpret and act on content at scale. You will help shape how the platform reasons about listings, profiles, messages and other user-generated content by building the next generation of Trust Content Understanding Models.
...
- The ideal candidate is a motivated and talented “full-stack” Data Scientist with strong applied ML intuition and a bias toward impact, who can own and drive forward challenging, high-visibility initiatives such as:
...
- Advance Airbnb’s content integrity capabilities by building Natural Language Processing (NLP) and LLM-based models that understand intent, policy compliance, quality and risk across listings, profiles, and user communications
...
- Develop high-performing models for detecting problematic or misleading content, including text classification, semantic similarity, information extraction and generative model-based reasoning for policy interpretation and enforcement
...
